title: newevablog pages not loading
wikitext:
[]: neweva.blog pages not loading             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sat May 04, 2013 6:27 pm Not sure if this is the right place to post about this, but recently I've come across links to pages from a "neweva.blog" site. Whenever I try to open these links, they just load for a really long time and then display a web page with no formatting. This happens in every browser I have. Has anyone else encountered a similar problem?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon May 06, 2013 1:33 am Just opened the main page and it works fine, did you try to clean the cache of your browser(s)? fc2.com sometimes gave me weird issues with unloaded pics, but usually it only regarded Firefox, Chrome seems to deal better with its bitchiness, and it never affected whole pages.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon May 06, 2013 11:20 am Opera's my main browser, and any page on that site that I try to open just loads until it says "Elements: 62/133" or some similar number, then just hangs there infinitely. Similar results with Chrome and Firefox, and I'm certain the caches for those browsers are almost if not completely clear.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon May 06, 2013 11:55 am Try to clear their cache anyway, it's possible that they are hanging over some error which happened when you first tried to open that blog with those browsers, clearing the cache or trying to access the blog from anonymous/private mode (which bypasses the cache) is the first thing to do.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon May 06, 2013 4:20 pm Next time, just go here: http://www.downforeveryoneorjustme.com/http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2301.html A number of things could be the issue, most likely not your browser, more likely your upstream provider has a DNS hiccup (which could last several days) where it fetched a bad record for the site you're going to and cached it, and you'll keep getting the bad record, resulting in your browser claiming that the site is down (because browser's are made to return generic/uninformative error messages). Less likely than that, you just have a bad route (again your upstream provider's problem) but those go away relatively quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon May 06, 2013 7:25 pm Yeah, that site says it's just me, and I figured as much when I saw everyone discussing the content on these pages. The problem exists with all of my browsers, even when their caches are clear, so I guess it's a problem with my ISP.
